title: aster subspicatus var. apricus
intro: Aster subspicatus var. apricus, also known as the apricot aster or western aster, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to western North America. The medicinal properties of this plant are primarily attributed to its flowers, leaves, and roots, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Aster subspicatus var. apricus include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
